exemplar.title: What is the probability of Argentina scoring from a penalty kick in the 2022 FIFA World Cup, and how reliable are their performances based on binomial distribution modeling?

detailedExemplarData.title: What is the probability of Argentina scoring from a penalty kick in the 2022 FIFA World Cup, and how reliable are their performances based on binomial distribution modeling?

subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Clear narrative structure with introduction, methodology, scenarios, hypothesis test, and conclusion
Logical progression between sections makes the argument easy to follow
Wellâdefined headings, subheadings, and tables aid navigation
weaknesses: Mis-labelled figures and minor digressions interrupt flow
Redundant repetition of success rates in multiple places
Some background material (e.g. sponsorship details) is not directly relevant
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments consistently placed the exploration in band 3 for coherence, organization, and relevance, indicating a well-organized and mostly concise presentation. Minor lapses such as mis-labelling and occasional digressions prevent a band 4 award, so a score of 3 is appropriate.

strengths: Use of basic symbols (n, p, pÌ, Ï) shows some awareness of mathematical notation
weaknesses: Frequent notation errors (e.g. misrendered binomial coefficients, undefined pâ²)
Inconsistent rounding conventions and mislabeled tables reduce clarity
Only tabular representations provided; no graphical support
Terminology (e.g. âbinompdfâ) is used without definition
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the student uses some correct symbols and tables, pervasive inconsistencies in notation, missing definitions, and the narrow variety of representations align more closely with band 1 descriptors. The communication is partially clear but lacks consistency, warranting a score of 1.

strengths: Independent choice of a realâworld problem with raw data collection
Personal narrative (lifelong fan, match watching) drives the topic selection
Creative application of binomial modelling to a sports context
weaknesses: Limited extension beyond standard course examples
Could further relate personal skills gained to future investigations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evidence of significant independent thinking and a clear personal approach places the work solidly in band 2 for personal engagement. While creative in context, it stops short of the novel initiative required for band 3.

strengths: Meaningful discussion of model limitations (independence, sample size)
Connection of results back to the research question and predictions
Acknowledgement of critique points such as small data set
weaknesses: Reflection remains at a descriptive level; deeper critical analysis (e.g. p-value, alternative models) is absent
Conclusion occasionally repeats earlier statements without new insight
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration contains meaningful reflection and some critique, matching band 2 descriptors. However, it does not achieve the depth and sustained critical analysis needed for band 3, so a score of 2 is justified.

strengths: Appropriate use of binomial distribution and one-proportion z-test at course level
Sound conceptual explanations of parameters and hypothesis-testing procedure
weaknesses: Multiple computational errors (wrong parameter in appendix, decimal misplacement)
Omission of normal approximation conditions and inconsistent rounding
Garbled binomial notation and contradictions between tables and text
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While relevant course-level mathematics is applied, significant accuracy issues and missing checks mean the mathematics is only partially correct. This aligns with band 2 for use of mathematics, so a score of 2 is appropriate.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student independently chose a real-world question, collected raw data by re-watching matches and checked it against FIFA statistics.  That demonstrates significant but not outstanding independent thinking, fitting band 2.

Explanations of binomial parameters, success/failure interpretation, and hypothesis-test procedure show sound conceptual grasp.  Misinterpretations are minor, so understanding is âgood in parts / mostly correctâ, aligning with band 3.

Most numeric answers are plausible, but several computational slips occur: the appendix uses 0.15 instead of 0.24 for shoot-out failures, the table entry 0.0244 contradicts the stated 0.244, and binomial coefficients are occasionally garbled.  These issues indicate the mathematics is âpartially correctâ, consistent with band 2.

Most sentences relate directly to the research question, and long digressions are limited.  Nevertheless, the narrative sometimes repeats information (e.g. success rates quoted in several places) and includes personal anecdotes that do not advance the mathematics.  This keeps the work ârelevant and mostly conciseâ rather than fully concise, meriting band 3.

Appropriate symbols such as n, p, \(\hat p\), \(\sigma\) and binomial coefficients are used, but notation is occasionally incorrect (e.g. \({^{s}_{s}}\) placeholder, inconsistent decimal precision).  Terminology like âbinompdfâ is stated without definition.  These inconsistencies place the work in the âsome appropriate languageâ category, i.e. band 2.

Explanations are generally understandable, but occasional errors (mis-copying probabilities, using 0.15 instead of 0.24 in the Appendix, wrong critical z-value notation) reduce consistency.  This yields âmostly clear with some consistencyâ, aligning with band 2.

Binomial distribution and one-proportion z-test are standard course-level techniques and are applied correctly to both open-play and shoot-out data.  This meets the rubric for ârelevant mathematics at course level usedâ and in places used effectively (e.g. cumulative probability), placing the work in band 3.

The student supplies two tables of probabilities and one summary table; no graphs or visual summaries (e.g. bar chart, normal curve for the z-test) are provided.  The tables do support the argument but representation is limited in variety and depth, matching the âsome appropriate representationsâ descriptor of band 2.

The conclusion section comments on the success of the model, discusses limitations (independence assumption, small sample, use of global averages) and reflects on prediction accuracy.  Reflection is meaningful but not deeply critical, earning band 2.

Applying binomial modelling and a one-proportion z-test to a sports context shows creativity and initiative beyond reproducing textbook examples, yet the mathematics stays at standard level with no novel extension.  Thus the strand fits âsignificant creativityâ, i.e. band 2.

The exploration follows a clear narrative: introduction â data â methodology â two scenarios of calculation â hypothesis test â conclusion.  Each section builds on the previous one and the argument is generally easy to follow.  Minor lapses (e.g. mis-labelled binomial coefficients or duplicated values) interrupt the flow, so the development is good rather than exemplary, aligning with the âcoherent and clear logical developmentâ description of band 3.

Some critique is present (e.g. acknowledging independence may not hold, limited sample size), but analysis of alternative models or statistical power is absent.  The depth corresponds to âmeaningful analysis with some critiqueââband 2.

The student clearly links findings back to the research question and personal predictions, indicating a connection between results and understanding.  However, the connection is not extensively developed to enhance wider mathematical comprehension, so band 2 is appropriate.

Headings, sub-headings and tables divide the piece sensibly; the reader can locate data, working and discussion quickly.  There is a recognisable introduction, body and conclusion, satisfying the rubric for a âwell organisedâ paper.  Transitions between major parts are present but not seamless; therefore band 3 is the best fit.

The personal narrative (life-long fan, interest in Tottenham, watching all matches) clearly drives the choice of topic and methodology.  This represents a âclear personal approachâ, characteristic of band 2.

annotations.comment: The title clearly identifies the subject and level, providing a strong first impression and helping orient the reader.
The research question sets a clear focus but the term âreliabilityâ could be defined more precisely (for instance, by specifying metrics or hypothesis statements).
The personal anecdote in the introduction demonstrates genuine engagement and connects emotionally to the explorationâs context.
This paragraph includes rich background but digresses into sponsorships and transfers; streamline to focus directly on how football metrics relate to penalty conversion.
The text mentions zâ0.905 but calculation implies 0.908; reconcile these minor numeric differences to avoid confusion.
The conclusion summarizes results but repeats earlier statements; consider reflecting on practical significance (e.g., coach decisions) to deepen the narrative.
The tabular data uses combined parentheses for shootout values without distinct headers; adding clear column labels for âMatchâ vs âShootoutâ improves clarity.
The notation p' is introduced for success probability but not defined; explicitly state what pâ² represents to avoid ambiguity in later calculations.
Combining n=5 and n=9 in a single list item blurs the distinction between scenarios; separate these into two clear bullet points linked to their respective contexts.
Referencing TI-84âs BINOMPDF function is useful, but a brief note on how it replicates the binomial formula will aid readers unfamiliar with calculator syntax.
In Scenario 1, the P(X=2) equation shows 0.244 but Table 1 lists 0.0244; this discrepancy likely stems from a misplaced decimal. Please correct for consistency.
The binomial formula application for P(X=0) yields 0.0000759, yet itâs rounded to 0.000075 in the table. Decide on a consistent rounding convention (e.g. 4 dp) and apply uniformly.
The binomial coefficient is misrendered as {^s_s} for P(X=3); correct it to $$\binom{5}{3}$$ to maintain proper mathematical notation.
Labeling the summary as âFigure 1â for a table may mislead readers; use âTable 1â or align labeling with content type for consistency.
The interpretation of P(Xâ¥4)=0.836 is correct but would benefit from an immediate comparison to Argentinaâs actual four-success performance to reinforce contextual relevance.
The percentage â0.0075%â from 0.000075 should be checked; ensure correct conversion (0.000075Ã100=0.0075%) and precise notation to avoid misinterpretation.
Table 2 reuses the same generic header as Table 1; consider customizing the caption and headers to denote this is shootout data for clearer distinction.
While the table presents probabilities effectively, adding a bar chart or other visual would enhance multiple representations and support pattern recognition.
The null hypothesis groups two different world averages under one pâ; define pââ=0.85 and pââ=0.76 to distinguish regular play and shootout tests.
The hypothesis testing section omits conditions for normal approximation (e.g., npââ¥5); include a brief check of these assumptions to validate the z-test.
The standard deviation is approximated as 0.160 but the z calculation uses 0.1597; choose a consistent rounding approach and apply the same value in both steps.
Interpreting the z-test result without reporting a p-value limits depth; including the p-value or confidence interval would strengthen critical analysis.
Suggest exploring future work such as alternative distributions or larger historical datasets to extend the investigation.
Comparing your calculated probabilities to real outcomes and acknowledging half of your prediction validates the model shows strong personal engagement.
The discussion of independence and sample size limitations shows meaningful critique; for deeper reflection, propose how you might model fatigue or psychological factors.
Your personal reflection on applying mathematical tools to your passion for football is compelling; to enhance, link specific skills learned to potential economic analyses you might pursue.
